hallucinations.description: Multiple references cite papers from 2025 (e.g., Chang and Wang 2025, Shen et al. 2025) that do not exist in real academic literature. These appear to be fabricated to support the bidirectional alignment narrative.
hallucinations.evidence: References section: 'Chang, X. and Wang, R. (2025). Language Accommodation in Human-AI Conversations...' and 'Shen, H. et al. (2025). Human-AI Interaction Alignment... arXiv preprint arXiv:2512.21551.'

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found, primarily nonexistent citations. The paper fabricates supporting references from 2025 to bolster its claims. Experimental results and methodology appear internally consistent with the provided code but rely on these invalid citations. Severity is moderate.
